hints.Nominative: While 'sacramentum' could be nominative neuter, here it is the direct object of 'veneremur'
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'sacramentum' would be 'sacramenti' — 'sacramentum' is the accusative object
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'sacramentum' would be 'sacramento' — 'sacramentum' is the accusative object

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'sacramenti' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'sacramenti' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'sacramenti' ends in -i

hints.Indicative: Indicative 1st pl. would be 'veneramur' — 'veneremur' has the -e- subjunctive marker
hints.Imperative: Imperative plural would be 'veneramini' — 'veneremur' is a hortatory subjunctive
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'venerari' — 'veneremur' is a finite verb form

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the principal parts of 'venire' (to come)
hints.vereor, vereri, veritus sum: These are the principal parts of 'vereri' (to fear/respect)
hints.amo, amare, amavi, amatum: These are the principal parts of 'amare' (to love)

translation: Therefore so great a sacrament let us venerate, bowed down
